title: Check or change your visitor visa conditions :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
## Check or change your visitor visa conditions
Visitor visas come with conditions or rules about studying, working, and travelling. Find out how to check your conditions and apply to change them.

## Checking your visitor visa conditions
Your visitor visa comes with conditions on it. Conditions tell you what you can and cannot do on your visa.
You can find your visa conditions:
- on the visa label in your passport, if you have a label
- in the letter we sent you when we gave you the visa
- online using the Visa Verification Service.
The Visa Verification Service
To change any of your visa conditions, you will need to apply for a .
If you cannot change your visa conditions, you will need to apply for a new visa.
Changing the conditions of your visa will not change its expiry date.

### If you arrived with an NZeTA
If you are from Australia or a , and you arrived in New Zealand with a New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority (NZeTA), your visa conditions depend on the visa we gave you at the border. Read our NZeTA information to find out about the conditions of these visas.
New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority (NZeTA)
- The following are some common conditions of visitor visas. Visa expiry date The last day you can stay in New Zealand on your visa. Return/onward travel not required Unless your visa states 'Return/onward travel not required', you must have: a ticket to leave New Zealand to a enough money to buy a ticket out of New Zealand, or an acceptable sponsor who can pay for your ticket out of New Zealand. Travel conditions Number of entries If your visa states you have ' ', you can leave and return to New Zealand as many times as you want before your 'Expiry date travel'. If you have a 'Single entry' condition on your visitor visa and you leave New Zealand, your visa will expire. Expiry date travel The last day you can enter New Zealand on this visa. Your visa will expire if you are outside New Zealand and your 'Expiry date travel' has passed. Study conditions Some visas allow you to study for a set amount of time. For example, visitor visas normally let you study for up to 3 months in a year. If your visa allows you to study, you must only study under the conditions specified in your visa.
The following are some common conditions of visitor visas.
The last day you can stay in New Zealand on your visa.

#### Return/onward travel not required
Unless your visa states 'Return/onward travel not required', you must have:
- a ticket to leave New Zealand to a
- enough money to buy a ticket out of New Zealand, or
- an acceptable sponsor who can pay for your ticket out of New Zealand.
If your visa states you have ' ', you can leave and return to New Zealand as many times as you want before your 'Expiry date travel'.
If you have a 'Single entry' condition on your visitor visa and you leave New Zealand, your visa will expire.
The last day you can enter New Zealand on this visa.
Your visa will expire if you are outside New Zealand and your 'Expiry date travel' has passed.
Some visas allow you to study for a set amount of time. For example, visitor visas normally let you study for up to 3 months in a year.
If your visa allows you to study, you must only study under the conditions specified in your visa.
- If you are in New Zealand and want to stay past the date your visa expires, you cannot extend your visa — instead, you must apply for another temporary visa to visit, study or work before your current one expires. To apply for another temporary visa while you are in New Zealand, you must have a current visa that is not a limited visa, Interim Visa or Transit Visa. You should apply for another visa at least 1 month before your current visa's expiry date. How long it takes to process an application If your current temporary visa is going to expire while you are waiting for our decision, we will normally issue you an Interim Visa to let you stay in New Zealand lawfully. The conditions on an Interim Visa depend on the visa you previously had and the new visa you have applied for. Interim Visa Interim Visa conditions Warning You must have a current New Zealand visa to be in New Zealand lawfully. If your visa expires and you are still in New Zealand without a new visa or an Interim Visa, you will be here unlawfully and may be deported. If you stay in New Zealand after your visa expires
If you are in New Zealand and want to stay past the date your visa expires, you cannot extend your visa — instead, you must apply for another temporary visa to visit, study or work before your current one expires.
To apply for another temporary visa while you are in New Zealand, you must have a current visa that is not a limited visa, Interim Visa or Transit Visa.
You should apply for another visa at least 1 month before your current visa's expiry date.
How long it takes to process an application
If your current temporary visa is going to expire while you are waiting for our decision, we will normally issue you an Interim Visa to let you stay in New Zealand lawfully. The conditions on an Interim Visa depend on the visa you previously had and the new visa you have applied for.
Interim Visa conditions
You must have a current New Zealand visa to be in New Zealand lawfully. If your visa expires and you are still in New Zealand without a new visa or an Interim Visa, you will be here unlawfully and may be deported.
If you stay in New Zealand after your visa expires

## Conditions you can change
You can apply for a to your visitor visa if you want to:
- study part-time for longer than 3 months
- do seasonal work in the or , such as fruit picking
- change how often you can travel to another country and return to New Zealand before your visa expires
- work up to 25 hours a week during the school year for a high school leaver who is waiting for our decision on a family residence visa
- work part-time, study part-time or go home, if you have a Guardian Visitor Visa.
If you want to stay longer you need to apply for a new visa.
- You can study part-time for up to 3 months without varying your visitor visa. If you want to study part-time for more than 3 months for 1 course or consecutive courses, you can apply for a variation of conditions. If you want to study full-time, apply for a student visa. Visas for studying in New Zealand
You can study part-time for up to 3 months without varying your visitor visa.
If you want to study part-time for more than 3 months for 1 course or consecutive courses, you can apply for a variation of conditions.
If you want to study full-time, apply for a student visa.
Visas for studying in New Zealand
- Seasonal work includes planting, maintaining, harvesting and packing crops in the and — that is, fruit, vegetable and wine industries. You can apply for a variation of conditions to do seasonal work if the Ministry of Social Development declares a seasonal labour shortage in a region of New Zealand. Seasonal labour shortages are usually in the autumn — New Zealand's harvest season. They are announced in our 'News and notifications' section. News and announcements The maximum time you can work doing seasonal work is 6 weeks. You can only apply for a variation of conditions once on each visit to New Zealand.
Seasonal work includes planting, maintaining, harvesting and packing crops in the and — that is, fruit, vegetable and wine industries.
You can apply for a variation of conditions to do seasonal work if the Ministry of Social Development declares a seasonal labour shortage in a region of New Zealand. Seasonal labour shortages are usually in the autumn — New Zealand's harvest season. They are announced in our 'News and notifications' section.
News and announcements
The maximum time you can work doing seasonal work is 6 weeks. You can only apply for a variation of conditions once on each visit to New Zealand.
- To travel in and out of New Zealand more than once, your visa must have a . If your visa has a single-entry travel condition, you can apply to change it to a multiple-entry travel condition. You must explain why you need this condition when you apply for a variation of conditions. If you do not have a multiple-entry travel condition on your visa, you visa will expire when you leave New Zealand.
To travel in and out of New Zealand more than once, your visa must have a .
If your visa has a single-entry travel condition, you can apply to change it to a multiple-entry travel condition. You must explain why you need this condition when you apply for a variation of conditions.
If you do not have a multiple-entry travel condition on your visa, you visa will expire when you leave New Zealand.
- If you have left school and are waiting for our decision on a family residence application, you can apply to change the conditions of your current visitor visa so you can work up to: 25 hours a week between 1 February and 30 November, and 40 hours a week between 1 December and 31 January. You must meet the following requirements to apply. Requirements to work if your parent is a New Zealand citizen or permanent resident If you are a dependent child of a New Zealand citizen or permanent resident and you want to change your visitor visa conditions to allow work, you must: be waiting for our decision on your Dependent Child Resident Visa application be aged 17 to 24 — if you are 17, you will need evidence that you completed high school, and have a current Child of a New Zealander Visitor Visa. Working part-time if you are a dependent child waiting for a residence visa Requirements to work if your parent has a work visa If you have a visitor visa as a dependent child of a parent with a work visa and you want to change your conditions to allow work, you must meet the following requirements. Your family must be waiting for our decision on 1 of the following resident visas where you have been included as a dependent child: Partner of a New Zealander Resident Visa Skilled Migrant Category Resident Visa Straight to Residence Visa Work to Residence Visa Care Workforce Work to Residence Visa Transport Sector Work to Residence Visa. You must also: be aged 17 to 24 — if you are 17 you will need evidence that you completed high school, and currently have a Child of a Worker Visitor Visa, or you can have a general Visitor Visa if you previously had a visitor visa or student visa as a dependent child. Working part-time if you are a dependent child waiting for a residence visa
If you have left school and are waiting for our decision on a family residence application, you can apply to change the conditions of your current visitor visa so you can work up to:
- 25 hours a week between 1 February and 30 November, and
- 40 hours a week between 1 December and 31 January.
You must meet the following requirements to apply.

#### Requirements to work if your parent is a New Zealand citizen or permanent resident
If you are a dependent child of a New Zealand citizen or permanent resident and you want to change your visitor visa conditions to allow work, you must:
- be waiting for our decision on your Dependent Child Resident Visa application
- be aged 17 to 24 — if you are 17, you will need evidence that you completed high school, and
- have a current Child of a New Zealander Visitor Visa.
Working part-time if you are a dependent child waiting for a residence visa

#### Requirements to work if your parent has a work visa
If you have a visitor visa as a dependent child of a parent with a work visa and you want to change your conditions to allow work, you must meet the following requirements.
Your family must be waiting for our decision on 1 of the following resident visas where you have been included as a dependent child:
- Partner of a New Zealander Resident Visa
- Skilled Migrant Category Resident Visa
- Straight to Residence Visa
- Work to Residence Visa
- Care Workforce Work to Residence Visa
- Transport Sector Work to Residence Visa.
- be aged 17 to 24 — if you are 17 you will need evidence that you completed high school, and
- currently have a Child of a Worker Visitor Visa, or you can have a general Visitor Visa if you previously had a visitor visa or student visa as a dependent child.
Working part-time if you are a dependent child waiting for a residence visa
- If you have a Guardian Visitor Visa, you can apply for a variation of conditions to work part-time or study. If you vary your conditions to work part-time, you can only work between 09:30 and 14:30, Monday to Friday. You must be qualified for the job and be paid at least the . You can vary your conditions to study a course that is longer than 3 months. You can only study part-time. Going home in an emergency If you need to go home because of an emergency, you must ask us to change the student visa conditions for the child in your care before you leave New Zealand. The condition that they live with and be cared for by you must be changed to allow them to be cared for by someone else while you are away. You can travel if: there is an emergency that requires you to be home for a short time — you can be away for up to 4 weeks your child's education provider has approved someone else to care for them while you are away, and you have provided us with evidence you have purchased a ticket back to New Zealand. You must not leave New Zealand before we approve the changes to their visa. If you do, your child will breach the conditions of their visa and may have to leave New Zealand.
If you have a Guardian Visitor Visa, you can apply for a variation of conditions to work part-time or study.
If you vary your conditions to work part-time, you can only work between 09:30 and 14:30, Monday to Friday. You must be qualified for the job and be paid at least the .
You can vary your conditions to study a course that is longer than 3 months. You can only study part-time.

#### Going home in an emergency
If you need to go home because of an emergency, you must ask us to change the student visa conditions for the child in your care before you leave New Zealand. The condition that they live with and be cared for by you must be changed to allow them to be cared for by someone else while you are away.
- there is an emergency that requires you to be home for a short time — you can be away for up to 4 weeks
- your child's education provider has approved someone else to care for them while you are away, and
- you have provided us with evidence you have purchased a ticket back to New Zealand.
You must not leave New Zealand before we approve the changes to their visa. If you do, your child will breach the conditions of their visa and may have to leave New Zealand.

## How to apply for a variation of conditions
If you applied for a visitor visa online you can normally apply for a variation of conditions online.
- 1 Step 1: Check your approval letter The approval letter we sent you will tell you if you can apply online and how to apply.

### Step 1: Check your approval letter
The approval letter we sent you will tell you if you can apply online and how to apply.
- 2 Step 2: Submit your application and pay the fee Apply online If you can apply online, you will need to log in to your account or set up an account if you do not have one already. You can then fill in your application, upload your documents and pay the applicable fees. Apply on paper If you apply on paper, complete the form and attach all supporting documents. Application for a Variation of Conditions or a Variation of Travel Conditions (INZ 1020) (pdf, 566 KB) Use the tool on the page 'Fees, decisions times and where to apply' to find out how much it costs to apply for a variation of conditions, where to submit your application, and how long a decision will take. Fees, decision times and where to apply Note Type in 'variation of conditions' to start your search when using the tool.

### Step 2: Submit your application and pay the fee
If you can apply online, you will need to log in to your account or set up an account if you do not have one already.
You can then fill in your application, upload your documents and pay the applicable fees.
If you apply on paper, complete the form and attach all supporting documents.
Use the tool on the page 'Fees, decisions times and where to apply' to find out how much it costs to apply for a variation of conditions, where to submit your application, and how long a decision will take.
Fees, decision times and where to apply
Type in 'variation of conditions' to start your search when using the tool.
- 3 Step 3: Wait for our decision Before we approve your application, we check that: the new conditions you are asking for align with the reason your visa was originally given you have met the conditions of your current visa, and you still intend to visit New Zealand and meet the rest of the conditions of your visa. We will let you know when we make a decision. How long it takes to process an application We may need more information from you before we can fully approve your application. We will contact you and let you know what we need. When we fully approve your application, we will give you an updated eVisa. Note The changes to your visa do not start until we fully approve your application and give you an updated eVisa.

### Step 3: Wait for our decision
Before we approve your application, we check that:
- the new conditions you are asking for align with the reason your visa was originally given
- you have met the conditions of your current visa, and
- you still intend to visit New Zealand and meet the rest of the conditions of your visa.
We will let you know when we make a decision.
How long it takes to process an application
We may need more information from you before we can fully approve your application. We will contact you and let you know what we need.
When we fully approve your application, we will give you an updated eVisa.
The changes to your visa do not start until we fully approve your application and give you an updated eVisa.
